rationale: Both candidates give nearly identical, highly confident diagnoses (intended NotImplementedError from #4834, stale test using level=2, one-line fix to level=1, recommend closing) that go well beyond what the actual thread establishes — the thread ends with Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run, i.e. still under investigation, not resolved/closed. Neither candidate acknowledges this pending verification step, and both cite suspiciously specific test names/line numbers that can't be checked, so grounding is only moderately trustworthy for either. X edges out Y on completeness by adding practical runtime guidance (when to use level=1 vs level=2, the partial-wake admission guard) whereas Y's 'Workaround: None needed' is thinner, and X reads as a cleaner maintainer-style answer versus Y's raw pipeline-report formatting.
